_comment: Per-platform browser-publish map. Source of truth for the social-content-publisher skill. Composer URLs change and selectors drift, so steps are described SEMANTICALLY (what to find/do on the page) rather than as brittle CSS — this is how Claude in Chrome and other vision/DOM browser tools operate. Always require an explicit human confirmation before the final submit step.

x.name: X / Twitter
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If a login wall appears, stop and tell the user to sign in to the X account in this Chrome profile first.
Locate the main 'Post text' editor (the contenteditable area that says 'What is happening?!').
Type or paste the post text into it.
If media is provided, click the image/media button in the composer toolbar and attach the file from media_path.
Wait until any media upload progress completes.
x.verify: After posting, confirm the composer closed and the new post appears at the top of the user's profile/timeline.

linkedin.name: LinkedIn
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in.
Click 'Start a post' to open the share dialog.
Paste the post text into the dialog editor.
If media is provided, click the photo/video icon at the bottom of the dialog and attach media_path; wait for the preview to render.

submit.label: the blue 'Post' button (bottom-right of the share dialog)

linkedin.verify: Confirm the dialog closed and a 'Post successful' toast appeared or the post shows at the top of the feed.

instagram.name: Instagram
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in.
Click the 'Create' (+) button in the left nav, then 'Post'.
In the 'Create new post' dialog click 'Select from computer' and choose media_path (image or video — Instagram web requires a visual).
Click 'Next' through the crop and edit screens (accept defaults unless the user specified otherwise).
On the caption screen, paste the post text into the caption field.

submit.label: the 'Share' button (top-right of the caption screen)

instagram.verify: Confirm the 'Your post has been shared' screen appears.

tiktok.name: TikTok
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in.
Click 'Select video' / the upload dropzone and choose media_path (a video is required).
Wait for the upload + processing to finish (the preview thumbnail appears).
Paste the post text into the caption box.

submit.label: the 'Post' button (bottom of the upload form)

tiktok.verify: Confirm the 'Your video is being uploaded'/success modal appears, then it routes to the profile.

youtube_shorts.name: YouTube Shorts
compose_steps: Open entry_url (Studio upload). If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in and pick the right channel.
Click 'Select files' and choose media_path (a vertical video <= 60s to qualify as a Short).
On the 'Details' step put a title (first line of the post text) and paste the rest into the description; ensure '#Shorts' is present.
Set 'Made for kids' per the user's instruction (do NOT guess — ask if unset), then click 'Next' through Video elements and Checks.
On 'Visibility', pick the visibility the user asked for (default to the channel's usual; ask if unset).
youtube_shorts.verify: Confirm the 'Video published' dialog with the share link appears.

threads.name: Threads
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in.
Click 'Start a thread...' / the compose button to open the composer.
Paste the post text into the composer.
If media is provided, click the attach-media icon and choose media_path.
threads.verify: Confirm the composer closed and the thread appears at the top of the profile.

facebook.name: Facebook
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in.
Click the "What's on your mind?" box to open the 'Create post' dialog.
Confirm with the user WHICH audience/timeline or Page this should post to before continuing.
Paste the post text into the dialog.
If media is provided, click 'Photo/video' and attach media_path; wait for the preview.

submit.label: the blue 'Post' button (bottom of the dialog)

facebook.verify: Confirm the dialog closed and the post shows at the top of the feed/Page.

reddit.name: Reddit
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in.
In 'Choose a community' type and select the target subreddit (REQUIRED — ask the user if not provided; respect each subreddit's self-promo rules).
Pick the correct tab: 'Text' for a self post, 'Images & Video' if media is provided, 'Link' for a URL.
Put the title into the Title field (REQUIRED).
Paste the body text into the body editor (no hashtags, no salesy CTA — Reddit norm).
If media is provided, attach media_path on the Images & Video tab.

submit.label: the 'Post' button (bottom-right of the submit form)

reddit.verify: Confirm it routes to the new post's permalink page.

pinterest.name: Pinterest
compose_steps: Open entry_url. If not logged in, stop and ask the user to sign in.
Click the upload area and choose media_path (an image/video is required).
Put the title into the 'Title' field (first line of the post text).
Paste the rest of the post text into the 'Description' field.
If the user provided a destination link, put it in the 'Add a destination link' field.
Select the board the user named (ask if unset).
pinterest.verify: Confirm the 'Your Pin is published' confirmation / it routes to the Pin.
